title: Fast Forge Boilerplate
description: Add your favorite forge mods on top of this, and dont get the performance hiccups!
body:
This modpack is a collection of mods that optimize forge performance.
It is made as a boilerplate, so you can later add all your favorite forge mods and be able to play without any lag!

All mods should be compatible, but if you are going to add more optimization mods, especially the ones that change how the game renders, I encourage you to check compatibility first. (or just test it and if it runs then happy face, either option is ok :D )

I recommed using Prism Launcher, as it is open source and very configurable and being very user-friendly.

Example with 32 chunks (took only about 30 seconds to generate them all)
![example](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/OYL78Y2G/images/ceb1ef28bafa41a5e78f6e366874dcb54be2f300.png)
My pc is somewhat standart but I think it will do fine even on the oldest of computers.
Also, i dont think you will be actually palying with 32 chunks, so i tested out with 12, and you can confidently run it with 1gb ram, which is half of the defautl size that minecraft allocates!

I even tried this on a HP x360 11 G3 EE (its a chromebook XD), which is insanely bad, boasting a Celeron N4120, and Intel uhd Graphics 600, and i got 60 fps on 12 chunks.
Im really surprised what modern performance mods can achieve, even on the worst hardware i could get (the above crapbook), it still runs fine!

Mods: Dynamic FPS, Embeddium, EntityCulling, FerriteCore, ImmediatelyFast, Ksyxis, Memory Leak Fix, ModernFix, Noisium, Radium, Saturn, Starlight.

You can submit bugs or suggestions [here](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeELpo_gqlwn4ftgP_tkB6I-rq6c6nyvs4BM07e89AKy3DI2g/viewform?usp=sf_link)
